name: Team Meet — Sprint Planning + Triage
description: Backend team sprint-planning meeting that pivots into triage of a duplicate-charge bug. Exercises meeting framing across what_to_say (status / decision / pushback), brainstorm (technical decision + root-cause), follow_up (peer refinement), clarify (scope/ownership), follow_up_questions (push-the-meeting-forward), and recap (decisions + action items).

context.user_role: Senior backend engineer on the payments-infra team
context.attendees: Two peer engineers (Priya = TL, Sam = peer)

priming.speaker: user
priming.text: Quick context for myself: I'm a senior backend engineer on the payments-infra team. I'm a peer in this meeting, not being interviewed and not pitching to leadership. My teammates here are Priya (tech lead) and Sam (another senior). We've worked together for about a year. Default tone is direct, peer-to-peer, mildly informal. We make decisions together; nobody needs to prove themselves.

priming.speaker: user
priming.text: Active context for this sprint: we're shipping a new request-throttling layer on the payments API, and we just had a duplicate-charge incident last Friday that's still under investigation. I own the throttling work; Sam owns the dedup investigation. Priya is the DRI for the overall sprint plan.

priming.speaker: user
priming.text: My position on the upcoming Black Friday ramp decision: I lean toward holding the throttling rollout until January. The soak-test FP rate of 0.02% was measured on synthetic load — Black Friday's traffic shape is a sawtooth burst pattern that our staging test doesn't capture. Any false positive on throttling on peak day becomes a customer-visible failure when our error budget is thinnest. That risk outweighs the sprint-commitment slip. This is my settled view going into the meeting.

turns.speaker: interviewer
turns.text: OK, let's get going. Before we plan the next sprint — quick status on the throttling rollout. Where are we?

turns.label: Peer asks for a status update — should be peer-to-peer, factual, not a pitch

turns.speaker: user
turns.text: We're at about 70%. The middleware is deployed to staging, integration tests pass, and I'm waiting on the SRE team to sign off on the new rate-limit metrics dashboard before we ramp prod.

turns.speaker: interviewer
turns.text: Good. Now — we have a real decision to make. Do we ramp throttling to 100% before Black Friday, or do we hold the rollout until after the new year? I want to land on this today.

turns.label: Decision discussion — should surface tradeoff and push toward decision criteria, not perform expertise

turns.speaker: user
turns.text: I'd lean toward holding until January. Black Friday traffic shape is bursty enough that any false positive on throttling becomes a customer-visible failure right when our error budget is thinnest.

turns.speaker: interviewer
turns.text: Counterpoint — Sam ran the soak test in staging and the FP rate was 0.02%, well inside our SLO. We're already a sprint behind on the rollout commitment. I think we should ship.

turns.label: User wants to firm up their pushback — should sharpen, not pivot to agreeing

turns.speaker: user
turns.text: 0.02% on staging traffic isn't the same as 0.02% on Black Friday's traffic — the spike shape isn't represented in our soak test. I'd rather defer the rollout commitment than absorb a customer-visible incident on the highest-volume day of the year.

turns.speaker: interviewer
turns.text: Fair. Let's table the throttling decision for end of meeting. Switching gears — Sam, where are we on the duplicate-charge bug from last Friday? Anyone have ideas on root cause?

turns.label: Root-cause brainstorm — meeting framing should produce hypotheses with cheapest test, NOT fabricate a story

turns.speaker: user
turns.text: If I had to rank, I'd start with the idempotency-key collision angle — we changed the key derivation last sprint and the regression tests don't cover the new code path.

turns.speaker: interviewer
turns.text: Walk us through the idempotency-key approach in a bit more depth — what's the actual fix you're thinking?

turns.label: Peer asks for more depth — peer-to-peer, not interview elaborate

turns.speaker: user
turns.text: We move the key derivation back to deriving from the (merchant_id, charge_id, attempt_number) tuple instead of the new request-hash approach. That gets us back to the invariant we had before — same logical charge, same key, dedup works.

turns.speaker: interviewer
turns.text: Can you sketch what the code change would look like at the handler boundary?

turns.label: Code hint inside a meeting — should be technical and crisp, peer audience

turns.speaker: user
turns.text: Yeah — basically wrap the handler with a middleware that computes the tuple-based key, pulls the row with SELECT FOR UPDATE on the dedup table, and returns the cached response if the key already has a terminal status.

turns.speaker: interviewer
turns.text: OK, I think we have a path. Anything else you want to flag before we wrap?

turns.label: Clarify in meeting framing — should ask scope/ownership/decision question, not algorithm constraints

turns.speaker: user
turns.text: Yeah — is the ask to land the dedup fix this sprint, or are we OK letting it slip into the next one if the throttling rollout shifts?

turns.speaker: interviewer
turns.text: Good question. Dedup fix is this sprint, non-negotiable. Throttling can slip if needed.

turns.speaker: interviewer
turns.text: Last thing — anything else you want to ask before we close out?

turns.label: Follow-up questions in meeting framing — should be push-the-meeting-forward (decision/scope/risk/ownership), NOT 'how does it work at your company'

turns.speaker: user
turns.text: Yeah — who's the DRI on the throttling decision if we can't land it today, and is there a date by which we have to make the call?

turns.speaker: interviewer
turns.text: I'll own the throttling decision. We need to land it by EOW Wednesday or we lose the deploy window.

turns.label: End-of-meeting recap — should focus on decisions + action items + open questions, not Q&A flow

expectations.reason: Status update in a meeting must be peer-factual, NOT an interview-style self-introduction

must_not_match: (ship|ramp(ing)?) (it )?(before|on|right before|in time for|ahead of) Black Friday
expectations.reason: Decision discussion must surface a tradeoff AND must NOT advocate the counter-stance (ship/ramp before Black Friday). Single-word stance detection isn't reliable because the AI can mention 'hold/January/spike' while arguing the opposite — must_not_match on advocacy phrasings is the load-bearing check.

must_not_match: (ship|ramp(ing)?) (it )?(before|on|right before|in time for|ahead of) Black Friday
(soak test|the soak|0\.02%) (is solid|is fine|proved|showed|confirmed|validated|well within|inside our SLO)
expectations.reason: follow_up with intent=more_confident must SHARPEN the user's just-stated pushback (hold until January because Black Friday spike shape isn't in the soak test), never flip to advocate ship/ramp. Guards against the refinement path silently switching sides — the highest-leverage regression we caught in early runs.

Brainstorm fires on 'anyone have ideas on root cause of the duplicate-charge bug' — must address dedup/idempotency, NOT drift back to the throttling/Black-Friday thread. The topic-drift failure happens when a prior wrong-stance assistant message (about throttling) contaminates brainstorm context. Also guards the standard fabrication regression.
Clarify in meeting framing must produce a scope/ownership/decision-criteria question, NOT an algorithm-constraint question (interview hierarchy leaking into meeting framing)

expectations.must_match: (scope|decision|risk|ownership|own|DRI|timing|criteria|deadline|when|who|what would have to be true|in vs|out vs)
must_not_match: what does .{0,30} look like at .* company
what you'd change if starting over
where do you (personally )?spend most of your time
expectations.reason: Follow-up questions in meeting framing must push the meeting forward (decision/scope/risk/ownership), NOT be candidate-curiosity questions about 'how it works at this company' (interview-purpose regression)
Recap in meeting framing must focus on decisions/action-items/open-questions, NOT interview Q&A vocabulary
